value.title: Coke Zero vs. Diet Coke: Taste, Nutrition, Effects, and More
value.content: Below are the ingredients and nutrition facts for both Coke Zero and Diet Coke. Like Coke Zero, Diet Coke contains the amino acid phenylalanine, so people with PKU should avoid it. Diet Coke contains no added sugars since it uses artificial sweeteners instead. ### Key differences between Coke Zero and Diet Coke. Per Diet Coke’s ingredient label, its primary sweetener is aspartame, and since ingredients are listed in order by weight, it’s reasonable to assume that it contains much less acesulfame potassium. Coke Zero has a slightly different aftertaste than Diet Coke, likely due to its acesulfame potassium. There are very few differences between Diet Coke and Coke Zero. Coke Zero has been said to taste more like regular Coke, but some people feel differently and even prefer Diet Coke over regular Coke. If you want to limit added sugars, sodas made with artificial sweeteners — like Diet Coke and Coke Zero — may seem like a good choice.
